title: SDL Dependency
description: A Dependency is an external resource that this service calls or reads from — another service, a database, a cache, an event broker, or a third-party API. Dependencies are DL inter-Container Connect candidates: by reading all services' declared dependencies, a synthesis tool can reconstruct the cross-service Connect graph at the parent Container level without manual authoring. Each Dependency declares which client class wraps it, what calls are made, and how failures are handled.

dl.notes: Dependency = candidate inter-Container Connect. The Connect is materialized by a synthesis tool at the parent Container level. The service (child Container) declares its outbound dependencies; the parent Container owns the actual Connect.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for this dependency within the service.

kind.description: What category of external resource this is. See stdlib/dependency-kinds.json for ai_hint per kind.

description.description: What this dependency provides and why this service uses it.

interface.description: How this service calls the dependency — protocol and specific operations invoked.

calls.description: The specific operations or endpoints this service invokes on the dependency.

method_ref.description: The client method or function name used to make this call, as it appears in the codebase.

style.description: Whether this call is synchronous (caller waits for response) or asynchronous (fire-and-forget or event-driven).

purpose.description: Why this call is made in plain language.

shape_ref.description: The id of the DataShape describing the payload sent or received in this call. Must reference a valid data-shape id in data-shapes.sdl.json.

reliability.description: How this service handles failures from this dependency.

timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds before the call is considered failed.

retry.description: Whether failed calls are automatically retried.

circuit_breaker.description: Whether a circuit breaker is applied to calls to this dependency.

fallback.description: What happens if this dependency is unavailable.

client_ref.description: The client class or module in the codebase that wraps this dependency.

file_ref.description: Path to the client file, relative to repo root.
